input.system:
You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

Orange and yellow sparks emit from the work area. The visual content remains identical across both frames with no movement or changes detected in this segment.

You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

compressed_segments.text: [0-2] Static title card shows orange text "Weekend" top and "Welder" bottom right on black background. Central illustration shows figure in blue helmet welding with orange/yellow sparks. [2-6] Cut to man "Ben McCall" in light blue shirt, jeans, black gloves before white garage door. Red fire extinguisher on wall right. Lower-third graphic shows name and logo, then adds "Weekend Welder". Scene widens to man behind wooden workbench with tools; red welding machine on table background left. [6-8] Man stands behind wooden workbench, hands near yellow ear muffs and gloves. Red welding machine visible left. Posture consistent looking at camera. [8-12] Black title card displays logo upper left. White text right reads "You Tube Episode 16", centered bottom text identifies project "Garden Trellis". Card persists static. [12-14] Transition to person in dark welding helmet and light protective jacket welding on table, producing bright white arc and sparks. Text "Getting Started" top, logo upper left. [14-20] A person in protective gear welds at a table, generating bright sparks. Overlays include the "Weekend Welder" logo (top left), "Getting Started" (white), "Your Project" (yellow), and "Garden Trellis" (large white letters, bottom). The scene cuts to black. [20-28] A man in a light blue shirt

A man in a light blue shirt stands behind a wooden workbench in front of a white garage door with white trim. He holds a curved metal framework upright with his left hand. Initially, his right hand rests on the lower left rod of the structure as he looks down at the metal pieces and then forward. He then lifts his right hand to gesture with an open palm towards the center of the framework. Finally, he lowers his right hand to touch the bottom left rod of the structure again, continuing to look down at the metal assembly.

[128-134] A man in a light blue shirt stands behind a wooden workbench, holding a curved metal framework upright with his left hand while his right hand rests on the lower left rod. He gazes at the assembly and camera against a white garage door background with white trim.
[134-138] Now wearing a dark welding helmet and protective gloves, the man leans over the workbench. A bright white and blue electric arc flashes at the contact point, scattering orange sparks as he manipulates the welding tool along the metal rods.
[138-142] The bright arc extinguishes, leaving small glowing orange spots on the rods and workbench. The man remains bent over the table, holding the electrode holder near the framework and adjusting his position.

[142-146] A man in a blue shirt and dark welding helmet bends over a wooden workbench, holding an electrode holder near a metal framework where small glowing orange spots persist on the rods following a weld. [146-152] He stands up and removes his helmet to hold the large curved metal framework upright with both hands, inspecting the joints and displaying the structure against a background of white garage doors with white trim. [152-156] A close-up shows a thin metal rod clamped horizontally in a grey vice on a wooden board, where a person wearing thick brown gloves adjusts the setup; a small orange clamp rests on the table to the left. [156-158] The individual, dressed in a light blue shirt and blue jeans, holds a long hollow metal tube and aligns it with the clamped rod, appearing to slide the tube over the rod's end.

[158-164] A person in a light blue shirt slides a long dark metal tube over a thin rod secured in a grey vice on a wooden workbench. They use the tube as a lever to bend the rod upwards into a sharp angle, then release the tool. [164-172] The individual, wearing a dark welding helmet and gloves, leans over to weld the metal rod. Bright white light and orange sparks emit from the electrode contact point as they secure the bend. [172-174] Following a page-peel transition, the man stands before white garage doors holding a large, curved metal trellis framework, displaying the completed welded assembly.

recent_thinks: [174-176] A man in a light blue shirt and jeans stands behind a wooden workbench, holding a large, curved metal framework upright. He grips the structure with both hands, displaying the welded assembly against a background of white garage doors with decorative trim while inspecting the metal piece.

[176-178] The man in a blue shirt and jeans continues to stand behind the workbench holding the curved metal framework. He shifts his grip, holding the left side with his left hand while raising his right hand to gesture towards or touch the central rods of the trellis, inspecting the assembly against the white garage doors.

[178-180] The man in the light blue shirt stands behind the workbench, gripping the curved metal framework with his left hand. He extends his right hand to point his index finger at a specific joint where the rods intersect on the upper left side of the structure, highlighting that area while looking at the assembly.

[180-182] The man in the light blue shirt stands behind the workbench, holding the large curved metal framework upright with his left hand. His right hand remains elevated, touching or gesturing near the central vertical rods of the structure. He looks towards the camera while displaying the welded trellis assembly against the white garage doors.

[182-184] The man in the light blue shirt continues to hold the large curved metal framework upright with his left hand. His right hand remains near the center rods. He looks directly at the camera, maintaining his position behind the wooden workbench against the white garage door background.

[20-28] A man in a light blue shirt

[100-108] A man in a light blue shirt and dark welding helmet welds metal rods on a wooden workbench. [108-114] The scene transitions via cross-dissolve to the man standing without a helmet. A man in a light blue shirt stands behind a wooden workbench in front of a white garage door with white trim. He holds a curved metal framework upright with his left hand. Initially, his right hand rests on the lower left rod of the structure as he looks down at the metal pieces and then forward. [128-134] A man in a light blue shirt stands behind a wooden workbench, holding a curved metal framework upright with his left hand while his right hand rests on the lower left rod. He gazes at the assembly and camera against a white garage door background with white trim.
[134-138] Now wearing a dark welding helmet and protective gloves, the man leans over the workbench. A bright white and blue electric arc flashes at the contact point, scattering orange sparks as he manipulates the welding tool along the metal rods.
[138-142] The bright arc extinguishes, leaving small glowing orange spots on the rods and workbench. The man remains bent over the table, holding the electrode holder near the framework and adjusting his position.

[142-146] A man in a blue shirt and dark welding helmet bends over a wooden workbench, holding an electrode holder near a metal framework where small glowing orange spots persist on the rods following a weld. [146-152] He stands up and removes his helmet to hold the large curved metal framework upright with both hands, inspecting the joints and displaying the structure against a background of white garage doors with white trim. [152-156] A close-up shows a thin metal rod clamped horizontally in a grey vice on a wooden board, where a person wearing thick brown gloves adjusts the setup; a small orange clamp rests on the table to the left. [156-158] The individual, dressed in a light blue shirt and blue jeans, holds a long hollow metal tube and aligns it with the clamped rod, appearing to slide the tube over the rod's end.

[160-162] A person wearing a blue shirt slides a long metal tube over a thin rod secured in a vice. They then use the tube as a lever to bend the rod upwards, creating a sharp angle in the metal piece while holding the tool steady.

[162-164] The person releases the metal tube lever, leaving the thin rod bent at an angle within the vice. Their hand moves away from the tool, while the shaped metal piece remains secured in the clamp on the wooden workbench. The background shows a blurred orange object.

[164-166] The hand and the long metal tube lever exit the frame. The thin metal rod remains clamped in the grey vice, now bent at a sharp upward angle. The rod stays stationary on the wooden workbench, with a blurred orange object visible in the background.

[166-168] A person in a light blue shirt and dark welding helmet leans over to weld the metal rod secured in the grey vice. Bright white light and orange sparks emit from the contact point as the electrode touches the metal on the wooden workbench.

[168-170] A person in a light blue shirt, jeans, and welding helmet continues welding a metal rod clamped in a grey vice. Bright white light and orange sparks emit from the arc on the wooden workbench. The individual wears gloves and leans forward, maintaining the weld on the rod.

[170-172] A person in a light blue shirt and welding helmet continues welding a metal rod secured in a grey vice. Bright white light and orange sparks fly from the contact point as the individual leans over the wooden workbench, holding the electrode steady against the metal.
